betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Molten Salt Reactor§f:
The Molten Salt Fission Reactor (nuclearcraft:salt_fission_controller) is NC's premier liquid-fueled fission reactor. Unlike the Solid Fission Reactor, the fuel is dissolved in a fluoride salt that circulates through the reactor core.

§6Multiblock Structure (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:310)§f:
The Salt Fission Controller is the heart of the reactor. Crafting recipe:
- Pattern "PMP / EFE / PMP" with P = plateElite, M = ingotZirconiumMolybdenum, E = ingotExtreme, F = steelFrame
- Yields 1 Salt Fission Controller

The full reactor includes:
- Salt Fission Casing (outer shell)
- Salt Fission Glass (transparent casing)
- Salt Fission Heater (heat exchanger to coolant loop)
- Salt Fission Coolant (NaK or FLiBe)
- Salt Fission Moderator (graphite or beryllium rods)
- Salt Fission Reflector (lead or beryllium)
- Salt Fission Irradiator Port (item processing)
- Salt Fission Vent (heat venting)

§6Fuel Forms (SaltFissionRecipes.java:18-26)§f:
The Salt Reactor uses fluid fuel dissolved in FLiBe (LiF-BeF₂) salt:
- §bTBU Fluoride FLiBe§f: Thorium breeder cycle
- §bLEU-233 / HEU-233 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Uranium-233 fuels
- §bLEU-235 / HEU-235 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Uranium-235 fuels
- §bLEN-236 / HEN-236 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Neptunium fuels
- §bLEP-239 / HEP-239 / LEP-241 / HEP-241 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Plutonium fuels
- §bMIX-239 / MIX-241 Fluoride FLiBe§f: MOX fuels
- §bLEA-242 / HEA-242 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Americium fuels
- §bLECm-243/245/247 / HECm-243/245/247 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Curium fuels
- §bLEB-248 / HEB-248 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Berkelium fuels
- §bLECf-249/251 / HECf-249/251 Fluoride FLiBe§f: Californium fuels

§6How It Works (SaltFissionRecipes.java:32)§f:
Each cycle:
- §bInput fluid§f: 1mB of fuel+FLiBe solution
- §bOutput fluid§f: 1mB of depleted fuel+FLiBe solution
- The depleted fuel can be sent to a §bFuel Reprocessor§f for actinide recovery
- Heat is transferred to the coolant loop via the Heater tiles
- FLiBe salt is recycled back into the reactor

§6Advantages Over Solid Reactor§f:
- §bOnline refueling§f: fluid fuel can be added without shutting down
- §bHigher temperatures§f: FLiBe salt operates at 700-900°C, vs ~400°C for solid fuels
- §bBetter neutron economy§f: liquid fuel allows continuous fission product removal
- §bEasier breeding§f: Thorium cycle is more efficient in molten salt form

§6Direct Descendants of Salt Fission Controller (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 4 items used in the controller recipe:
- Elite Plating (nuclearcraft:part:3) — top-tier plate
- Zirconium-Molybdenum Alloy Ingot — high-temperature alloy
- Extreme Alloy Ingot — toughest NC alloy
- Steel Chassis — common frame

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30038 (Elite Plating) and q30043 (Solid Fission Reactor).

§eTip§f: Start with LEU-235 Fluoride FLiBe — it's the easiest salt fuel to produce (LEU-235 from Isotope Separator + FLiBe from Chemical Reactor).

§eTip§f: For breeding, run the Thorium cycle: TBU Fluoride FLiBe breeds into U-233, which can be recirculated as LEU-233/HEU-233.

§cWarning§f: Molten FLiBe salt is highly toxic and corrosive. A leak in the salt loop can melt through machine frames and contaminate the surrounding area. Always run the reactor in a contained, well-ventilated room.
